8 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Somebody asks this question on Reddit roughly every day, and the answers are always the same two flavors. Either the boom is over and you missed it, or it is the earliest of early days and you are practically a founder. Nobody brings numbers. So I went and got them, because the question is actually arithmetic and not philosophy, and the arithmetic turns out to be far stranger than either camp admits.
The world population sits at about 8.30 billion as of July 2026, per Worldometer’s mid-year figure. That is the denominator for everything below, and it does more work in this article than any of the numerators do.
The 900 million number is smaller than it sounds
Press enter or click to view image in full size

ChatGPT reported 900 million weekly active users in February 2026, up from 400 million a year earlier. That figure has held steady in OpenAI’s public statements since. The app crossed one billion monthly actives in mid-2026, the fastest in recorded history.
Divide 900 million by 8.30 billion and you get 10.8 percent.
